sanskrit: न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद् यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम्। अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम्॥
transliteration: na hi prapaśyāmi mamāpanudyād yac chokam ucchoṣaṇam indriyāṇām | avāpya bhūmāvasapatnam ṛddhaṃ rājyaṃ surāṇām api cādhipatyam ||
literal_meaning: I do not at all see what could dispel this sorrow that is withering my senses, even if I should obtain on earth a prosperous, unrivalled kingdom or the supreme lordship over the gods.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confesses that no worldly attainment—including absolute power—can soothe the existential grief that is depleting his life-energy. He implicitly surrenders to the need for a deeper, trans-personal source of guidance.
psychological_state: Acute existential depletion; recognition that ego-based solutions (status, power, achievement) cannot regulate overwhelming affect; first glimmer of therapeutic alliance (turning toward Krishna).
trigger_scenario: A high-performing executive who has 'everything' still wakes up with a hollow dread and finally texts a therapist: 'I can’t fix this on my own.'
anchor_text: Worldly power cannot heal the soul’s grief; turn within for lasting peace.
